chroma.headline: Chroma
chroma.subtitle: Cosmetic consumables that can activate light effects on Chroma-enabled armor and weapons.

sections.textBlock:
Chroma is a type of cosmetic consumable similar to Ornaments, which can be consumed by slotting it into a piece of gear with the matching color of Chroma node. This node can be randomly reset to another color (or potentially the same color) for 100 Glimmer, which does not refund any Chroma already slotted in. Chroma can be acquired through Treasure of Ages, Sterling Treasures, or dismantling certain Chroma-enabled gear such as the original Spektar Armor.

Chroma was originally introduced in the April Update for The Taken King, alongside Sterling Treasures and the Spektar Armor sets. Queen's Wrath and Prison of Elders weapons introduced in this update are the only weapons with Chroma channels. Initially only Red, Blue, Yellow and White colors were available, but this was later expanded with Orange, Green, and Magenta all of which work on all Chroma gear.

These engrams may drop from enemies, from reward chests within activities, from the Activity Rewards end screen, or be purchased from any Cryptarch in exchange for Legendary Marks. There are distinct engrams for each of the weapon and armor slots, excluding Artifacts and Ghost Shells. The contents of Legendary Engrams decrypt into a maximum power of 390, and can be increased further via Infusion.

Legacy Engrams are exclusivey purchased from Xûr, Agent of the Nine. Each weekend he will have one slot available to purchase for 29 Strange Coins. These engrams only decrypt into Exotic gear from Year 1, excluding the raid Exotics Mythoclast and Necrochasm, and operate on a knock-out system based on the Exotic Collection kiosks. They will provide you something you have never had before, after which they will decrypt at random.

The primary reasons to purchase would be for collection, acquisition of a non-reprised weapon such as Pocket Infinity, or to unlock blueprints of reprised Year 2 or 3 versions in the Collection Kiosks. Ownership of a Year 1 Exotic will count as discovery of that Exotic, allowing the later version to be purchased at the kiosk. This method excludes any Year 2 or Year 3 reprised weapon that is acquired from a Quest (such as Thorn) or from certain unique sources (such as raid weapons or Ice Breaker).

Legendary Engrams from the release era of *House of Wolves* remain available exclusively through the Activity Rewards screen of the matchmade level 28 version of the Prison of Elders. These engrams only come from this activity end screen, not the reward chests at the end the activity. The potential contents remain as they were when first released, meaning they decrypt into Vanguard, Crucible, Dead Orbit, Future War Cult, and New Monarchy items from *House of Wolves*.

Although labeled here as Legacy, they are identical to other Legendary Engrams in your inventory. They award the same Crypto-Archeology Reputation for decryption as other Legendary Engrams, and do not have the duplicate protection of Legacy Exotic Engrams. Engrams from different release eras are presented on a separate decryption prompt from newer engrams; should you lose track of which is which, this is your only indicator of their identity prior to decryption.

Also available from the same end screen rewards are Rare Engrams named Decoherent Engrams. These decrypt into rare gear from *House of Wolves* and have the usual small chance to decrypt upward into a Legendary item from these tables.

Basic Vanguard bounties to gain experience and reputation, refreshing with 6 available each daily reset. These can be acquired from the Bounty Tracker frames in any of the three social spaces, who share the same inventory. All of these bounties can be progressed in any activity other than the Crucible and social spaces.

Available options will always include 1 Reconnaissance bounty for a destination; while the objective uses the word Patrol, these can be advanced in Patrol, Story Missions, Strikes, or Raids on the destination. Bounties have no expiration date, and can be abandoned if you do not wish to complete them.

Basic Crucible bounties to gain experience and reputation, refreshing with 6 available each daily reset. These can be acquired from the Bounty Tracker frames in any of the three social spaces, who share the same inventory. All of these bounties can be progressed in any Crucible modes, including in Private Matches but excluding Sparrow Racing.

Elite Vanguard bounties provide an additional source of experience and reputation, with 6 available each daily reset. These bounties are all centered around earning Medals or scoring points, which can only be done in either the SIVA Crisis Heroic Strike playlist or the Weekly Nightfall.

Requires *Rise of Iron* campaign and quest completion. Each week Commander Zavala will offer the Sunrise bounty, and 2 other bounties from this list. The Sunrise bounty can only be completed through the Weekly Nightfall, while the other bounties can also be completed in the SIVA Crisis Heroic Strike playlist. The Sunrise bounty offers a weapon reward, and the other bounties offer armor and potentially cosmetics.

subjectDetailCards.title: Clean Up
subjectDetailCards.description: Get 150 enemy kills with your fireteam during a SIVA Crisis Strike to earn Brutal Efficiency Medals.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "It's not difficult to be efficient when you're working together." —Zavala

subjectDetailCards.title: Make a Rainbow
subjectDetailCards.description: Rapidly earn Primary, Special, and Heavy Streak Medals to earn Rainbow Medals.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "Every weapon type in the Guardian arsenal has a use. Get familiar with them." —Zavala

subjectDetailCards.title: Merciless
subjectDetailCards.description: Get 9 rapid enemy kills to earn Rampage Medals in SIVA Crisis Strikes.

subjectDetailCards.title: Simply Perfect
subjectDetailCards.description: Complete SIVA Crisis Strikes without any member of your fireteam dying to earn Flawless Medals.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "A perfect Guardian is difficult to come by. A fireteam of perfect Guardians is almost an impossibility. Well done." —Zavala

subjectDetailCards.title: Strike Elite
subjectDetailCards.description: Earn Gold Tier Achieved, Silver Tier Achieved, or Bronze Tier Achieved Medals.

subjectDetailCards.title: Sunrise
subjectDetailCards.description: Earn a Gold Tier Achieved Medal in the Nightfall before time expires.
subjectDetailCards.quote: "The enemy won't wait for you to be ready. To excel, you'll need sheer force of will." —Zavala

sections.textBlock: This bounty is the exclusive source of the Year 3 version of Ice Breaker, which has a chance to drop as an additional reward alongside your legendary weapon, and is capped to drop at 370 even if you have a higher Light Level. Otherwise the Legendary weapons that can drop are the pool of weapons from the Vanguard Reputation Package, as well as Year 3 Faction weapons.

Each week the Crucible Quartermaster, Arcite 99-40, will have 5 bounties for you to complete by scoring kills in any Crucible mode with heavy weapons, two types of primary weapon, and two types of special weapon. Each rewards a guaranteed Legendary weapon of the same type required to complete the bounty.

The pool of rewards are weapons from Year 2 and 3 of: Crucible vendor weapons, Crucible match reward weapons, and the otherwise unobtainable weapons from the Classic Playlist match rewards, Iron Banner, and Trials of Osiris. Trials weapons exclude the Adept versions.

Each week Lord Shaxx provides 2 bounties corresponding to currently available 6V6 Crucible modes, requiring multiple objectives to be completed across multiple match completions.

The Unknown Rewards may seem confusing, but has a very specific reward potential. Notably, it includes the chance to acquire select Crucible, Iron Banner, and Trials of Osiris cosmetics. Cosmetic rewards are an additional drop if they happen, and respect the knock-out system of the Collection kiosks. However the core, guaranteed reward is one of these: a Legendary weapon from a limited Crucible Match reward pool, an Exotic weapon or armor, or a bundle of 17-20 Strange Coins. Estimated reward odds from a sample size of 112 are: ~60% Legendary Weapon, ~30% Exotic Gear, ~10% Strange Coins.

Potential Exotic drops exclude any weapons with explicit sources such as Quests or Raids. Due to the knock-out system, data confirming available cosmetic rewards is limited. The Emblem list is mostly confirmed. The listed Shaders and Vehicles are combined from observation and source data.

summaryRewards.rewardText: (Guaranteed 1 of) Legendary Weapon | Exotic | 17-20 Strange Coins
(Possible 1 of) Strange Coin | Mote of Light
